no_wind.explanation: In calm air, flight reflects release and disc stability with minimal external correction.
tips: Use this condition to prioritize form and angle control.

headwind.explanation: Headwind gets under the flight plate and adds lift, making the disc behave as if it is moving faster — more understable, turning right more. Throw nose-down and low to cut under the wind. Nose-up releases stall and hyzer out early.
tips: Use more overstable than normal, throw low with a nose-down angle.

tailwind.explanation: Tailwind pushes on top of the flight plate, reducing lift and making the disc behave as if it is moving slower — more overstable, losing glide and fading sooner. Drop one stability class from what you'd throw in calm conditions. Throw slightly higher to compensate for the reduced glide.
tips: Drop down in disc stability and speed class; throw higher than normal.

right_to_left.explanation: Right-to-left crosswind hits the bottom of the flight plate on a flat or hyzer release, adding lift and extra left push — amplifying the natural RHBH fade. On anhyzer it hits the top, reducing glide. R-to-L works with the natural disc fade.
tips: Use more overstable than calm conditions; aim right and let the combined forces work left.

left_to_right.explanation: Left-to-right crosswind hits the bottom of the flight plate on an anhyzer release, adding lift and right push — this is the distance-record wind. On a hyzer release it hits the top, reducing glide and pushing the disc down. Riding it with anhyzer generates extra carry.
tips: For distance, ride it with slight anhyzer. For control, use flat or hyzer to reduce wind exposure.

headwind_right_to_left.explanation: Headwind adds lift and understable effect; R-to-L also pushes left and adds lift. Both forces promote left movement — strong overstable disc essential for straight shots. Going right is extremely difficult.
tips: Maximum overstable needed; aim right; throw low and nose-down.

headwind_left_to_right.explanation: Headwind adds lift and understable effect; L-to-R also pushes right. Both forces promote right movement — strong overstable disc essential for straight or left shots. Right shots are easy but hard to control.
tips: Maximum overstable for straight/left shots; throw low and nose-down to cut wind.

tailwind_right_to_left.explanation: Tailwind reduces lift and makes disc more overstable; R-to-L also pushes left. Both forces promote fade and left drift. Drop in stability and aim well right to compensate for the combined left movement.
tips: Drop in stability; aim right; throw slightly higher than normal.

tailwind_left_to_right.explanation: Tailwind reduces lift and makes disc more overstable; L-to-R also pushes right. Both forces push right — unstable discs can flip violently. Use overstable to fly straight; understable only for intentional right shots.
tips: Use overstable for straight shots; aim left; throw slightly higher than normal.

uphill.explanation: Uphill throws lose forward speed and require stronger commitment to hold intended flight.
tips: Add controlled power to maintain line through the climb.

flat.explanation: Flat ground keeps carry and finish closest to the intended release profile.
tips: Treat flat conditions as baseline calibration.

downhill.explanation: Downhill throws carry farther and can exaggerate late movement and skip risk.
tips: Use less power and prioritize landing control.

straight.explanation: Straight lines require balanced turn and fade with minimal lateral drift.
tips: Pick profiles that hold center line the longest.

short_left.explanation: Short-left lines prioritize dependable fade at controlled speed.
tips: Choose profiles with reliable finish over raw distance.

short_right.explanation: Short-right lines need controlled right movement without late over-fade.
tips: Favor touch and angle discipline over speed.

long_left.explanation: Long-left lines balance carry first, then strong finish.
tips: Aim to keep glide before fade phase starts.

long_right.explanation: Long-right lines need turn that holds long enough before correction.
tips: Protect against early fade by matching stability and angle.

s_curve.explanation: S-curve lines need early turn plus late recovery for full shape.
tips: Match speed and angle so both phases appear.

distance.explanation: Distance lines maximize carry while controlling late movement.
tips: Prioritize clean launch and full extension.

putting.explanation: Putting lines prioritize repeatability, entry angle, and finish control.
tips: Reduce variability and commit to one release.

hyzer.explanation: Hyzer release biases flight toward earlier left finish and lower turn exposure.
tips: Use hyzer to stabilize risky high-turn setups.

flat.explanation: Flat release keeps flight neutral and reveals the profile's natural stability behavior.
tips: Use flat when you want the clearest read on disc behavior.

anhyzer.explanation: Anhyzer release increases right movement and delays left finish.
tips: Use controlled speed to avoid unwanted burn-over.
